name: Kofikampu
source: Price 2025 (Afobaka); Les Premiers Temps map p.36
notes: Ndyuka village on Sara Creek; church shown tipping over as reservoir rose (photo in Price 2025). Submerged 1964.

name: Lebidoti
source: Price 2025; Les Premiers Temps map p.36
notes: Ndyuka Sara Creek village; oracle GaanTata attempted to stop the rising water. Submerged 1964.

name: Kiikipandasi
source: Price 2025; Les Premiers Temps map p.36
notes: Ndyuka Sara Creek village; oracle directed multiple relocations as water rose. Submerged 1964.

name: Pisiang
source: Price 2025; Les Premiers Temps map p.36

name: Makambikiiki
source: Price 2025; Les Premiers Temps map p.36
notes: One of first two villages to flood (with Watyibasu), six months after dam closed Feb 1964.

name: Watyibasu
notes: First village to flood alongside Makambikiiki, ~mid-1964.

name: Ganzee
notes: Largest submerged Saamaka village, ~2,000 inhabitants. Residents relocated to Nieuw Ganzee downstream.

name: Bedoti
source: Price 2025; Les Premiers Temps map p.36
notes: Village of Captain Cesar Adjako. Residents moved to transmigration village Kayapaati. Submerged 1964.

name: Nyu Kapasilee
notes: Saramaka village on Suriname River. Submerged 1964.

name: Matyakiiki
notes: Saramaka village on Suriname River. Submerged 1964.

name: Kaashliki
notes: Saramaka village on Suriname River. Submerged 1964.

name: Nuylonde
notes: Saramaka village. Submerged 1964. Name variant: Nyu Lombe on map p.36.

name: Lombe
notes: Saramaka/mixed village on Sara Creek. Submerged 1964.

name: Kapas Ikee
notes: Kwinti village on Sara Creek. Submerged 1964.
